data.body: PASS 1 review (G6 — approach soundness): no findings for this criterion. Approach is sound and fit-for-purpose: a hand-written, source-verified API reference directly solves the named gap (no single reuse-surface reference for building ON the signing/runtime/prompt machinery). Mechanism is complete (signatures + invariants + worked new-gate example + CI anti-drift AC7). All named symbols verified to exist at the claimed locations (signing.sign_manifest/verify_signature/verify_record/head_sha, runner.RunRequest fields mode/execution_mode/output_schema/extra_tools, Runner.run/preflight, get_runner, workflow.executor.run_workflow, prompts.resolve_prompt, findings.validate_structured). Considered alternatives (auto-generated/docstring-only API docs) and judged the hand-written doc defensibly superior for the LLM-audience prose, custody/invariant narration, and worked examples the plan requires. Positive rationale present in the Why section.
